NKJV.1: Blessed is the man Who walks not in the counsel of the ungodly, Nor stands in the path of sinners, Nor sits in the seat of the scornful;
NKJV.2: But his delight is in the law of the LORD, And in His law he meditates day and night.
NKJV.3: He shall be like a tree  Planted by the rivers of water, That brings forth its fruit in its season, Whose leaf also shall not wither; And whatever he does shall prosper.
NKJV.4: The ungodly are not so, But are like the chaff which the wind drives away.
NKJV.5: Therefore the ungodly shall not stand in the judgment, Nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous.
NKJV.6: For the LORD knows the way of the righteous, But the way of the ungodly shall perish.

ESV.1: Blessed is the man  who walks not in the counsel of the wicked, nor stands in the way of sinners, nor sits in the seat of scoffers;
ESV.2: but his delight is in the law  of the LORD, and on his law he meditates day and night.
ESV.3: He is like a tree planted by streams of water that yields its fruit in its season, and its leaf does not wither. In all that he does, he prospers.
ESV.4: The wicked are not so, but are like chaff that the wind drives away.
ESV.5: Therefore the wicked will not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous;
ESV.6: for the LORD knows the way of the righteous, but the way of the wicked will perish.

NASB.1: How blessed is the man who does not walk in the counsel of the wicked, Nor stand in the path of sinners, Nor sit in the seat of scoffers!
NASB.2: But his delight is in the law of the LORD, And in His law he meditates day and night.
NASB.3: He will be like a tree [firmly] planted by streams of water, Which yields its fruit in its season And its leaf does not wither; And in whatever he does, he prospers.
NASB.4: The wicked are not so, But they are like chaff which the wind drives away.
NASB.5: Therefore the wicked will not stand in the judgment, Nor sinners in the assembly of the righteous.
NASB.6: For the LORD knows the way of the righteous, But the way of the wicked will perish.

NLT.1:
Oh, the joys of those who do not
follow the advice of the wicked,
or stand around with sinners,
or join in with mockers.
NLT.2:
But they delight in the law of the LORD,
meditating on it day and night.
NLT.3:
They are like trees planted along the riverbank,
bearing fruit each season.
Their leaves never wither,
and they prosper in all they do.
NLT.4:
But not the wicked!
They are like worthless chaff, scattered by the wind.
NLT.5:
They will be condemned at the time of judgment.
Sinners will have no place among the godly.
NLT.6:
For the LORD watches over the path of the godly,
but the path of the wicked leads to destruction.

WEB.1: Blessed is the man who doesn’t walk in the counsel of the wicked, nor stand on the path of sinners, nor sit in the seat of scoffers;
WEB.2: but his delight is in Yahweh’s  law. On his law he meditates day and night.
WEB.3: He will be like a tree planted by the streams of water, that produces its fruit in its season, whose leaf also does not wither. Whatever he does shall prosper.
WEB.4: The wicked are not so, but are like the chaff which the wind drives away.
WEB.5: Therefore the wicked shall not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous.
WEB.6: For Yahweh knows the way of the righteous, but the way of the wicked shall perish.

CSB17.1:
How happy is the one who does not
walk in the advice of the wicked
or stand in the pathway with sinners
or sit in the company of mockers! ⓐ
CSB17.2:
Instead, his delight is in the LORD’s instruction,
and he meditates on it day and night. ⓑ
CSB17.3:
He is like a tree planted beside flowing streams [1]
that bears its fruit in its season ⓒ
and whose leaf does not wither. ⓓ
Whatever he does prospers. ⓔ
CSB17.4:
The wicked are not like this;
instead, they are like chaff that the wind blows away. ⓕ
CSB17.5:
Therefore the wicked will not stand up in the judgment, ⓖ
nor sinners in the assembly of the righteous.
CSB17.6:
For the LORD watches over the way of the righteous, ⓗ
but the way of the wicked leads to ruin. ⓘ

AMP.1:
Blessed [fortunate, prosperous, and favored by God] is the man who does not walk in the counsel of the wicked [following their advice and example],
Nor stand in the path of sinners,
Nor sit [down to rest] in the seat of scoffers (ridiculers).
AMP.2:
But his delight is in the law of the LORD,
And on His law [His precepts and teachings] he [habitually] meditates day and night.
AMP.3:
And he will be like a tree firmly planted [and fed] by streams of water,
Which yields its fruit in its season;
Its leaf does not wither;
And in whatever he does, he prospers [and comes to maturity].
AMP.4:
The wicked [those who live in disobedience to God's law] are not so,
But they are like the chaff [worthless and without substance] which the wind blows away.
AMP.5:
Therefore the wicked will not stand [unpunished] in the judgment,
Nor sinners in the assembly of the righteous.
AMP.6:
For the LORD knows and fully approves the way of the righteous,
But the way of the wicked shall perish.

MSG.1:
How well God must like you—
you don't hang out at Sin Saloon,
you don't slink along Dead-End Road,
you don't go to Smart-Mouth College.
MSG.2:
Instead you thrill to God's Word,
you chew on Scripture day and night.
MSG.3:
You're a tree replanted in Eden,
bearing fresh fruit every month,
Never dropping a leaf,
always in blossom.
MSG.4:
You're not at all like the wicked,
who are mere windblown dust—
MSG.5:
Without defense in court,
unfit company for innocent people.
MSG.6:
God charts the road you take.
The road they take is Skid Row.

NIV.1:
BOOK I
Psalms 1–41
Psalm 1
Blessed is the man
who does not walk in the counsel of the wicked
or stand in the way of sinners
or sit in the seat of mockers.
NIV.2:
But his delight is in the law of the Lord,
and on his law he meditates day and night.
NIV.3:
He is like a tree planted by streams of water,
which yields its fruit in season
and whose leaf does not wither.
Whatever he does prospers.
NIV.4:
Not so the wicked!
They are like chaff
that the wind blows away.
NIV.5:
Therefore the wicked will not stand in the judgment,
nor sinners in the assembly of the righteous.
NIV.6:
For the Lord watches over the way of the righteous,
but the way of the wicked will perish.

NRSVCE.1:
Happy are those
who do not follow the advice of the wicked,
or take the path that sinners tread,
or sit in the seat of scoffers;
NRSVCE.2:
but their delight is in the law of the LORD,
and on his law they meditate day and night.
NRSVCE.3:
They are like trees
planted by streams of water,
which yield their fruit in its season,
and their leaves do not wither.
In all that they do, they prosper.
NRSVCE.4:
The wicked are not so,
but are like chaff that the wind drives away.
NRSVCE.5:
Therefore the wicked will not stand in the judgment,
nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous;
NRSVCE.6:
for the LORD watches over the way of the righteous,
but the way of the wicked will perish.

NET.1: How blessed is the one who does not follow the advice of the wicked, or stand in the pathway with sinners, or sit in the assembly of scoffers!
NET.2: Instead he finds pleasure in obeying the LORD’s commands; he meditates on his commands day and night.
NET.3: He is like a tree planted by flowing streams; it yields its fruit at the proper time, and its leaves never fall off. He succeeds in everything he attempts.
NET.4: Not so with the wicked! Instead they are like wind-driven chaff.
NET.5: For this reason the wicked cannot withstand judgment, nor can sinners join the assembly of the godly.
NET.6: Certainly the LORD guards the way of the godly, but the way of the wicked ends in destruction.
